component.name: Badge
component.description: Static category label pill. Outline: border only, transparent background. Filled: neutral-subtlest background. Purely presentational — no selection state and no interaction semantics. Distinct from Chip, which is an interactive selection toggle.
component.figmaNodeId: none — scaffolded from the ROADMAP Phase 5d spec; no dedicated Figma node.

when: Labelling a course or card with a static category (e.g. Design, Development)
Showing a non-interactive level or status (e.g. Beginner, New, Certified)
Displaying a compact metadata token in a card or list row

patterns.name: card-category-row
patterns.description: A row of static category badges in a card's metadata area.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using Badge as a filter the user can toggle
antiPatterns.reason: Badge is display-only with no selection state or click semantics.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Chip, which renders a button with aria-pressed for selection.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using Badge as a clickable action
antiPatterns.reason: Badge renders a <span> and carries no action weight.

antiPatterns.scenario: Putting long sentences inside a Badge
antiPatterns.reason: Badge is a compact pill with nowrap text; long content breaks the layout.

purpose.outline: Bordered pill on transparent background. Use on plain surfaces where a fill would compete with surrounding content.
purpose.filled: Neutral-subtlest background, no visible border. Use on elevated or busy surfaces where a fill reads more clearly than a thin border.

states.description: Static label pill; the only state. No hover, focus, or disabled states — Badge is non-interactive.

accessibility.notes: Renders a plain <span> with visible text — no ARIA role needed; the label is read inline as text content. Not focusable and not interactive. If the badge conveys meaning beyond the adjacent content (e.g. a status not otherwise stated), make the label text explicit rather than relying on colour alone.
